zotero.abstractNote: Variational quantum algorithms (VQAs) are leading candidates for demonstrating quantum advantage on near-term quantum devices. A major bottleneck for VQAs is the measurement cost, i.e., the number of quantum measurements required to estimate the cost function. We introduce an adaptive optimizer, called SPSA-F (Simultaneous Perturbation Stochastic Approximation with Frugal measurements), that reduces the measurement cost of VQAs. SPSA-F is based on the SPSA algorithm but incorporates a measurement-frugal strategy that reuses measurement data to estimate the gradient. We prove that SPSA-F converges to a local minimum of the cost function under standard assumptions. We numerically demonstrate that SPSA-F significantly reduces the measurement cost compared to standard SPSA and other gradient-based optimizers for various VQA problems, including the Variational Quantum Eigensolver and the Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm.
